heightBehavior._rule: ALL cards in the same list/grid MUST have identical height regardless of data presence.
heightBehavior._reason: Cards with originalPrice show strikethrough → taller. Cards without → shorter. Mixed heights in a row = bad UX.
heightBehavior.description: Always reserve vertical space for ALL optional content rows (strikethrough, rating, deliveryLabel) even when the data is nil/null. Use invisible placeholders (opacity 0 or transparent spacer with same height) so every card in a list occupies the same total height.

platformGuidance.ios: Use .hidden() modifier on placeholder views to reserve space without rendering content. Or use .opacity(0) on a dummy element with matching font/size.
platformGuidance.android: Use Modifier.alpha(0f) on a placeholder Composable, or Spacer(Modifier.height(X.dp)) matching the line height of the missing row.
platformGuidance.alternative: Set a fixed minHeight on the card content section (not the whole card) calculated as: titleHeight(2 lines) + ratingHeight + strikethroughHeight + priceHeight + deliveryHeight + spacing. This ensures uniform cards without invisible elements.

affectedRows: strikethroughPrice — absent when originalPrice is null
ratingBar — absent when rating is null
deliveryLabel — absent when not a new arrival or no delivery info

productFeatured._widthNote: 160 is the SVG design reference at 390px screen. In code, use this as a fixed width for scroll items — scroll allows overflow.
svgContentOrder: image (1:1, with wishlist button overlay top-right)
ratingBar (stars + review count, NO rating value)
strikethroughPrice (above sale price, if originalPrice exists)

productStandard._widthNote: 170 is the SVG reference. In code, use flexible width: Modifier.weight(1f) / GridItem(.flexible()). The grid calculates actual width from (screenWidth - padding - gutter) / 2.
svgContentOrder: image (1:1, with wishlist button overlay top-right)
ratingBar (stars + review count, NO rating value)
